title: Segfault on query involving deeply nested aggregate views
key_bug_features: query involving deeply nested aggregate views
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, age INTEGER, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE comments (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER, content TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY(user_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO users (name, age) VALUES ('Alice', 30), ('Bob', NULL), ('Charlie', 25);
INSERT INTO comments (user_id, content) VALUES (1, 'First comment'), (2, 'Second comment'), (1, NULL), (3, 'Third comment');
SELECT user_id, COUNT(content) AS comment_count FROM comments GROUP BY user_id HAVING comment_count > 0 ORDER BY comment_count DESC;
